Fiduciary Law Attorney?S Petition For Construction Of Trust Terms Re: Payment Of Probate Expenses
Off Calendar Nov-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PES-17-301322, Estate of Marilyn Mallen; and
2) PTR-18-302018, Marilyn Mallen Trust.

Appearances:
Regina Wang for petitioner and administrator Daniel Mallen, who was also present.
Emilie Calhoun, petitioner and former attorney for Daniel Mallen.
John Boessenecker for co-trustees Kelli Mallen, Mary Mallen, and Kevin Mallen, who were also present.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) First and Final Accounting and Report of Administrator, filed by Daniel Mallen; and 2) Petition for Construction of Trust Terms re: Payment of Probate Expenses, filed by Emilie Calhoun.

The Court confirmed review of the filed papers. Ms. Wang stated she had nothing further to add. Ms. Calhoun highlighted unresolved issues from her opposition, and pointed out inconsistencies in the estate inventory, unexplained removal of assets, and lack of clarity on how the settlement benefitted the estate. She also stated that the dismissal was ambiguous and noted that the settlement documents were executed by Daniel Mallen individually rather than as administrator.

The Court asked Mr. Boessenecker how they would get around Probate Code Section 19001. Mr. Boessenecker argued that the statute could not override the trust's express terms and cited Haggerty v. Barton (2024).

Following discussion, the Court took the matters under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Maya Morohoshi, CSR 564, maya.morohoshi5@gmail.com (D204)

Petition For Order: 1)Removing Trustees Kelli And Mary Mallen Andor Suspending Their Powers; 2) Appointing Thomas Mallen To Serve As Co-Trustee With Kevin Mallen; 3) Or In The Alternative Appointing A Professional Fiduciary To Serve As The Sole Trustee; 4) Bringing The Trust Under Court Supervision; And 5) Requiring Bond
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Continued To Nov-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, in court, for Daniel Mallen, Objector; John Boessenecker, via ZOOM, for Mari Mallen, Kelli Mallen, in court, and Kevin Mallen, Petitioners.

The court begins by discussing with Ms. Calhoun the similarities of her petitions in the related trust matter specifically, the petitions filed in June and July of this year. Ms. Calhoun provides oral testimony to distinguish the two petitions. The court then asks Mr. Boessenecker to clarify as to what capacity he is representing his clients. Mr. Boessenecker reports that he is representing his clients as fiduciaries. There is discussion between the parties and court regarding the pending lawsuit. Mary Mallen reports that she is currently interviewing attorneys to represent her in that action. The court then confirms with Mr. Boessecker that he intends to file an opposition to the demurrer in the trust matter. The parties and the court continue to discuss the Moncada property and the parties confirm that the plan for some time has been to sell the property. The court then queries the parties regarding settlement discussions. Mary Mallen reports that the parties have mediated in the past and is open to doing so again. Kelli Mallen agrees that settlement discussions may help resolve this matter. Additional history of the matter is provided by Kevin Mallen and Daniel Mallen and all parties report that it would be beneficial to the parties to attempt to discuss settlement and avoid moving to the next stage of litigation. The court reminds the parties to continue to uphold their fiduciary duties.

After additional discussion, the Petition in this matter and the related trust matter are continued to Nov-29-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The parties are ordered to contact ADR Administrator Elizabeth Kelber by Oct-06-2021 to schedule a Mediation and complete the mediation by Oct-22-2021. A Status of Mediation is set for Nov-01-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John E. Boessenecker, for and with Petitioner Kelli Mallen and Mary Mallen; Emily Calhoun and Elina Protich for and with Daniel Mallen.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that the Court has the authority to grant the petition, either under the holding of the Hagstead case or independently under the Court's power to impose a constructive trust. He asserted that there was no reason to delay, and that if the petitioner was objectionable as trustee, the Court should appoint a neutral to serve as trustee. Ms. Calhoun responded that the trust was not funded during the life of the trustor, so the property remained part of the trustor/decedent's estate and needed to be probated. She further represented that she and her client had requested being provided with accounting documentation and bank records, which the petitioner had failed to provide. Mr. Boessenecker replied that the co-trustees, as holders of the decedent's powers of attorney, had the power to create and fund the trust under PC 4264(a). The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)
